behaviors.description: The request sent to the external API always includes the complete list of valid taxonomy labels.
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is the handoff between taxonomy config/request builder/API client (cross-module wiring), not pure local logic. A unit test of only a formatter could miss dropped labels during module integration. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: it only checks request payload composition, not real HTTP/OS behavior (so not System) and not model output quality (so not Agentic/Workflow).
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier module to a stub API client that captures outbound payload; invoke classify() with a taxonomy and assert the captured request contains the full label set exactly once before dispatch.

behaviors.description: When the API returns a label that is not in the taxonomy, the module returns "unknown" instead of forwarding the unexpected value to the caller.
behaviors.justification: This behavior is local normalization/validation logic: map out-of-taxonomy label to "unknown". No real infra, model behavior, or multi-module orchestration is required to expose the bug. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: it asserts output mapping for an already-received label, not transport/retry/live API semantics.
behaviors.test_description: Call the normalization/response-mapping function (or smallest callable boundary) with taxonomy [legal, technical, financial, other] and raw label "medical"; assert final label is "unknown".

behaviors.description: For identical raw API responses, the module's normalization step always produces the same final label.
behaviors.justification: Determinism of normalization is a pure function/property test at one object/function boundary. Integration or higher would not catch additional failure modes for this specific behavior text. Disqualifier for higher levels: it explicitly scopes to identical raw responses and normalization step only.
behaviors.test_description: Invoke normalization repeatedly (or property-test with multiple repetitions) on the same raw response fixture and assert identical final label each run.

behaviors.description: On repeated live API calls for the same document and taxonomy, the returned label remains consistent unless the provider response itself changes.
behaviors.justification: Behavior explicitly requires repeated live AI API calls and consistency of returned labels, which depends on real model/provider behavior over time. A mock would hide true variability and cannot validate this boundary. Workflow is disqualified by text: this is repeated single-step classification calls, not a multi-agent multi-step pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Run N repeated calls (same doc/taxonomy) against the real provider in a controlled window; record provider raw response and final label each run; assert label stays constant whenever raw provider response is unchanged, and only changes when provider response changes.

behaviors.description: Request construction enforces the configured token budget before dispatch (for example truncate/reject according to policy).
behaviors.justification: Failure boundary is module interaction between token-budget policy, request constructor, and dispatch gate (state handoff before send). Unit-only tests of token counting can miss dispatch-path bugs. Higher levels are disqualified by behavior text: checks pre-dispatch enforcement, not live network/OS behavior or model judgment quality.
behaviors.test_description: Use classifier wired with tokenizer/budget policy and stub dispatcher; feed over-budget input and assert configured policy outcome (truncate or reject) occurs and outbound request respects budget before dispatcher is called.

behaviors.description: Provider-reported token usage for a typical document stays within the configured per-call budget.
behaviors.justification: This requires a real provider call and real provider-reported usage fields; stubs can mask true provider accounting/model-side tokenization behavior. Integration is disqualified because behavior text is about actual provider-reported usage, not just local routing/formatting. Workflow is disqualified: single model call metric check, not multi-step agent pipeline.
behaviors.test_description: Execute classification against the real provider with representative document(s); read provider usage metadata from response; assert reported total/prompt/completion tokens are <= configured per-call budget.

behaviors.description: When the API signals that the request rate is too high, the module waits and retries rather than returning an error to the caller.
behaviors.justification: Primary failure boundary is retry orchestration across modules (API adapter + backoff/wait + caller contract). This can be caught with controlled in-process stubs and clock control; real OS/network is not required for this specific behavior. Agentic/Workflow are disqualified by text: no dependence on model output quality or multi-agent sequencing.
behaviors.test_description: Wire classifier to a stub API client that returns rate-limit error then success, with controllable clock/sleeper; assert module performs wait/backoff, retries expected times, and returns successful label instead of surfacing immediate error.
